questions.question: What is a queue data structure?
options: A) A last-in-first-out (LIFO) data structure
B) A first-in-first-out (FIFO) data structure
C) A data structure that stores only unique elements
D) A data structure that stores elements in a random order
questions.explanation: A queue is a data structure that follows the FIFO principle, where the first element added to the queue is the first one to be removed.

questions.question: What is the main difference between a circular queue and a standard queue?
options: A) A circular queue can only store one element
B) A circular queue can only store two elements
C) A circular queue is the same as a standard queue
D) A circular queue is a queue where the last element connects to the first element
questions.explanation: A circular queue is a type of queue where the last element connects back to the first element, allowing it to wrap around.

questions.question: What is the advantage of using dynamic arrays to implement a queue?
options: A) Dynamic arrays are more efficient than linked lists
B) Dynamic arrays can store only one element
C) Dynamic arrays can handle large amounts of data
D) Dynamic arrays are more secure than static arrays
questions.explanation: Dynamic arrays can handle large amounts of data and are more memory-efficient than other data structures like linked lists.

questions.question: What is the purpose of multiple stacks and queues?
options: A) To implement a single stack or queue
B) To implement multiple stacks and queues for complex operations
C) To implement a single queue for simple operations
D) To implement a single stack for simple operations
questions.explanation: Multiple stacks and queues are used to implement complex operations such as implementing a single queue, and to handle multiple operations simultaneously.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of adding an element to the end of a queue in a dynamic array?
options: O(n)
O(log n)
O(n log n)
questions.explanation: The time complexity of adding an element to the end of a queue in a dynamic array is O(1) because the array can be resized dynamically.
